name: Cornmeal Crepes with Berries
recipeIngredient: Vanilla low-fat yogurt OR Light/regular sour cream
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, mix strawberries and sugar; set aside.
2. In a blender, whisk milk, eggs, flour, cornmeal, and vanilla until no lumps remain.
3. Place a flat-bottom 7-8 inch-wide frying pan or crepe pan over medium-high heat.
4. When the pan is hot, add 1/4 teaspoon butter and swirl to coat the surface.
5. Pour in 1/4 cup batter all at once; quickly tilt the pan so the batter flows over the entire surface (don't worry if there are a few holes).
6. Cook until the surface is dry and the edges are lightly browned, about 1 minute.
7. Turn the crepe with a spatula and cook the other side until browned.
8. Turn out onto a plate; keep warm. Repeat to cook the remaining crepes, stirring the batter thoroughly before cooking each one (cornmeal sinks) and stacking them as made.
9. Spread 1 side of each crepe lightly with jam; fold into quarters.
10. Serve crepes with berries and yogurt to taste.
Makes 8 to 10 crepes.
Per serving with berries: 334 calories, 10g protein, 55g carbohydrates, 8.5g fat (3.7g saturated), 173mg cholesterol, 101mg sodium.
